mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is stronger than its recent baseline.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS value before decision date was 404596.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day SPX return spreads versus lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the labor deterioration becomes observable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE value before decision date was 8.2
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if triggered average returns are not below non-triggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains near the zero lower bound, real policy rates fall and inflation-risk premia rise, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises above its prior 12-month pace
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS value before decision date was 0.16
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 trigger is not followed by lower 60-day SPX returns than other zero-rate periods, or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals rising factory output and stronger cyclical earnings, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as analysts revise cash-flow expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO value before decision date was 97.2853
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum fails to generate higher 60-day SPX returns than negative momentum in out-of-sample windows, or if Rank IC is negative for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in housing starts signals strengthening construction demand, credit transmission, and durable-goods spillovers, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST value before decision date was 708.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside breakouts are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if average triggered returns are below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms genuine labor-market tightening, raising household income and earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change exceeds 100 thousand and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS value before decision date was 133088.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and unemployment-decline signal does not outperform either single-series signal over 20 trading days, or if its hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate tightens discount rates and financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity valuations adjust to the policy shock.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS value before decision date was 0.16
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by nonnegative average 5-day SPX returns, or if the negative return response is not distinguishable from zero at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with elevated CPI inflation identifies a stagflationary demand regime that compresses margins and valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is below 1 percent and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is above 2.5 percent
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 value before decision date was 13491.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-GDPC1-growth and high-CPI trigger is followed by higher 60-day SPX returns than normal-growth regimes, or if the interaction coefficient is positive in walk-forward estimation.
